title: hieracium pallescens subsp. pertenuatum
intro: Hieracium pallescens subsp. pertenuatum is a subspecies of the Hieracium genus, commonly known as hawkweed, a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. It is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, treating wounds, and potentially aiding in the management of certain infections. Traditionally, the plant is prepared through infusions or decoctions to create herbal remedies, such as teas and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
